chapter: JEF P101 – A Triumph of Surgery
title: A Triumph of Surgery – Easy SAQs

questions.question: Who is Tricki?
questions.answer: Tricki is a small pet dog owned by Mrs Pumphrey.

questions.question: Who is the owner of Tricki?
questions.answer: Mrs Pumphrey is the owner of Tricki.

questions.question: What kind of person was Mrs Pumphrey?
questions.answer: Mrs Pumphrey was a rich and overindulgent pet owner.

questions.question: Why did Tricki fall ill?
questions.answer: Tricki fell ill due to overfeeding and lack of exercise.

questions.question: What was the profession of the narrator?
questions.answer: The narrator was a veterinary surgeon.

questions.question: What advice did the vet give Mrs Pumphrey?
questions.answer: He advised her to reduce food and give Tricki more exercise.

questions.question: Why was Tricki taken to the surgery?
questions.answer: Tricki was taken to the surgery to control his diet and routine.

questions.question: What treatment was given to Tricki?
questions.answer: Tricki was given a strict diet and regular exercise.

questions.question: Did Tricki undergo surgery? Give reason.
questions.answer: No, Tricki did not undergo surgery because his illness was cured naturally.

questions.question: How long did Tricki stay at the surgery?
questions.answer: Tricki stayed at the surgery for about two weeks.

questions.question: What change was seen in Tricki after treatment?
questions.answer: Tricki became active, healthy, and energetic.

questions.question: Why is the title 'A Triumph of Surgery' ironic?
questions.answer: The title is ironic because no surgery was actually performed.

questions.question: What did Mrs Pumphrey send to the vet?
questions.answer: She sent eggs, wine, and brandy to the vet.

questions.question: Why did Mrs Pumphrey send these items?
questions.answer: She believed they would help Tricki regain strength.

questions.question: How did the vet feel about the items sent by Mrs Pumphrey?
questions.answer: He was tempted by the luxury items.

questions.question: What message does the story convey?
questions.answer: Excessive love without discipline can be harmful.

questions.question: What role does humour play in the story?
questions.answer: Humour makes the message gentle and interesting.

questions.question: How did the vet treat Mrs Pumphrey?
questions.answer: He treated her with kindness and patience.

questions.question: What was Tricki’s condition at the beginning?
questions.answer: Tricki was dull, listless, and overweight.

questions.question: What helped Tricki regain his strength?
questions.answer: Exercise and playing with other dogs helped him regain strength.

questions.question: What quality of the vet cured Tricki?
questions.answer: The vet’s common sense cured Tricki.

questions.question: How did Mrs Pumphrey react after Tricki’s recovery?
questions.answer: She was very grateful and emotional.

questions.question: What does Tricki’s recovery symbolise?
questions.answer: It symbolises the importance of discipline and moderation.

questions.question: What is the main theme of the prose?
questions.answer: The danger of overindulgence.

questions.question: Why is the story relevant today?
questions.answer: Because excessive care without discipline still causes problems.
